title: Xtra Fluffy Ukrainian Cassaba Dumplings
description: A hearty and comforting Ukrainian dish featuring cassaba, perfect for a cold winter's night, this flexitarian-friendly recipe is a twist on traditional Ukrainian cuisine.
instructions: In a large bowl, combine the grated cassaba, flour, warm water, yeast, sugar, and salt, and mix until a smooth dough forms.
Knead the dough for 10 minutes until it becomes elastic and shiny.
Place the dough in a greased bowl, cover it with a damp cloth, and let it rise in a warm place for 1 hour.
Preheat the oil in a deep frying pan to 350 degrees Fahrenheit.
Once the oil is hot, use a spoon to drop small balls of the dough into the oil, being careful not to overcrowd the pan.
Fry the dumplings until they are golden brown and puffed up, about 5 minutes on each side.
Remove the dumplings from the oil with a slotted spoon and place them on a paper towel-lined plate to drain excess oil.
Serve the dumplings hot, topped with fried onions if desired.
Let the dumplings cool completely on a wire rack before storing them in an airtight container in the refrigerator.
Reheat the dumplings in the oven or microwave before serving.
